message:
fix(freeze): remove cpool traversal to fix bytecode dispatch collisions

The registerFunctionAndCpool function was causing bytecode pointer collisions
when multiple different functions had the same name@line key (e.g., anonymous@6614).
When a nested function was looked up by name@line, the wrong frozen function
could be registered for its bytecode pointer if another function shared the key.

This caused closures to be called with wrong closure_var_count (e.g., 0 when
the frozen function expected 1), resulting in undefined variable access.

Fix: Remove cpool traversal entirely. Functions are now only registered by
bytecode pointer when first called via name-based dispatch. This ensures each
function is registered with its correct frozen function, with no collisions.

- Lodash: 84/129 tests pass (up from crashing immediately)
- Closure pattern test: Still passes
